results.title: Gaya Junction Set for Major Modernization and Upgrades
results.description: Gaya Junction in Bihar is undergoing a major transformation with extensive modernization efforts aimed at enhancing passenger experience. The upgrades include the installation of escalators and lifts, improved waiting areas, and enhanced security measures. These changes are expected to make travel more convenient, especially for elderly and differently-abled individuals. With its proximity to the religious hub of Bodh Gaya, the revamped station is anticipated to boost tourism and the local economy. Construction is progressing rapidly, promising a modernized station ready to serve travelers soon.

results.title: Late selling extends FBM KLCI's losing streak to three sessions
results.description: KUALA LUMPUR: Last-minute selling erased earlier gains and dragged the FBM KLCI lower for a third consecutive session on Wednesday, despite the benchmark spending most of the day in positive territory.

results.title: Two dead in Mexico City as World Cup celebrations turn tragic
results.description: At least two people died in Mexico City during huge World Cup celebrations after Mexico beat Ecuador to reach the last 16. The win sparked nationwide joy but overcrowding led to suffocation incidents, raising concerns over crowd safety.

results.title: SAPS turns to SIU for help with lifestyle audits on senior management
results.description: The South African Police Service has formally requested the Special Investigating Unit to assist in conducting lifestyle audits on senior management, amid ongoing concerns about corruption and compliance within the organisation.

results.title: Hundreds of migrants stranded as repatriation delays continue
results.description:
Hundreds of migrants stranded as repatriation delays continue Estelle.Bronkhorst Wed, 07/01/2026 - 11:25

MIDDELBURG - Nearly 200 Zimbabwean nationals have been stranded at a taxi rank in Middelburg, Mpumalanga, for the past three days.

The group, which includes women, children and the elderly, has been waiting to return home.

However, taxi operators have refused to transport them until payment is made upfront.

The migrants say living on the streets has been difficult and are pleading for assistance.

Meanwhile, scores of Malawian nationals remain stranded at a temporary repatriation processing centre in Musina, Limpopo.

Many have spent up to three nights sleeping on buses or on the ground while waiting to return home.

At the same time, a deadly crash near the Beitbridge Border Post has renewed concerns over driver fatigue and the safety of repatriation transport.

The driver of a bus transporting passengers from KwaZulu-Natal to a repatriation camp in Musina was killed when the vehicle overturned on the N1. Eleven other people were injured.

The assistant driver says he did everything he could to prevent the crash, but was unable to avoid it.

results.title: Outsourced workers at Bagerhat 250-bed hospital stage sit-in, demand job retention
results.description: Protesters claimed they had served the hospital for the past two years in accordance with all government rules and regulations but the newly appointed contractor had decided to replace experienced staff with newly recruited employees without providing any valid reason.

results.title: NRC: Humanitarian Funding for Afghanistan Falls to Its Lowest Level
results.description: The Norwegian Refugee Council (NRC) has said that Afghanistan is among the countries receiving the least humanitarian funding this year. In a report released on Wednesday, July 1, the NRC said that, for the first time, Afghanistan has been included on its list of the world’s most neglected displacement crises. results.title: Family of three-year-old injured in crocodile pit say they are facing an ‘extremely challenging time’
results.description: The family of the three-year-old boy injured in a crocodile pit have thanked the zoo staff who rescued him and those who are caring for him in hospital, as they said their “attention remains focused on his recovery” in a statement released through Cambridgeshire Police.
